steps.title: Identify the Lord Chancellor's claim about the crowd
steps.content: The Lord Chancellor remarks on “such unanimity” — directly characterizing the crowd as united.

steps.title: Find where his claim contradicts the evidence
steps.content: Yet the passage shows the crowd is disunited: different people shout different things (“Bread!” and “Taxes!”), and nobody knows what they really want. The Lord Chancellor's comment on unanimity contradicts this disunity directly. Choice D states exactly this paradox: he “describes the crowd as being united, even though the crowd clearly appears otherwise.” The other choices describe contradictions that don't actually exist in the passage. This matches choice D — D is correct.
